title: What do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai
display_title: What do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai
wikitext:
[]: What do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai             Quote Post by shinji kun » Thu May 19, 2011 8:37 am Just a curious question, what do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai?? For me, it's the best mellow song I ever heard! :') How about you?Please leave a reply here :)

[]: Re: What do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu May 19, 2011 8:41 am Shinji kun wrote: Just a curious question, what do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai?? For me, it's the best mellow song I ever heard! :') How about you?Please leave a reply here :)

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu May 19, 2011 12:33 pm Maybe he meant this? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hj_9Y0suyYo

[]:             Quote Post by theobject » Thu May 19, 2011 3:02 pm Or maybe this? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qvxIEQ6lrNs

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu May 19, 2011 4:25 pm Actually, he means this .

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu May 19, 2011 7:26 pm Relevant . Win. Needless to say I think it's a wonderful song. Very emotional in the movie too, although not as much as the Asuka scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu May 19, 2011 7:27 pm Yeah, I saw that in one of the comments on the video. I was freaking out when I realized it was true.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Thu May 19, 2011 9:21 pm ...um, it's a good song? The structure is sublime if you listen to the 5 minute version? Yeah, I got nothing. Pretty difficult to talk about a song like this on its' own merits if you ask me. Still wondering on how it's related to the "Song of Music" though.

[]: Re: What do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai             Quote Post by symbv » Thu May 19, 2011 10:34 pm Shinji kun wrote: Just a curious question, what do you think about Tsubasa O Kudasai?? For me, it's the best mellow song I ever heard! :') How about you?Please leave a reply here :)

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Fri May 20, 2011 3:09 am i think its ok, and i think the unit 01 raping unit 03 scene in 2.0 shouldnt of used this song for BGM

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri May 20, 2011 3:17 am juaxoo wrote: and i think the unit 01 raping unit 03 scene in 2.0 shouldnt of used this song for BGM

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Fri May 20, 2011 3:23 am Ornette wrote: It doesn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri May 20, 2011 7:41 am Tsubasa is slightly less win than before when I found out that Susan Boyle did an english version of it. Well, a song to the same tune and lyrics with roughly the same meaning. But even so....

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri May 20, 2011 11:36 am Hearing that uber hyper other version of it ss very jarring... or does it just prove Hayashibara-sans Talent that she was able to turn THAT into what we heard in the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri May 20, 2011 12:02 pm I believe it is time to let all of you listen to what the very original version of Tsubasa wo Kudasai in 1971 sounds like. Check: http://youtu.be/7in5rpYU584 And as I said, this is a song very often gets sung in Japan school, so you all should also know how this song might sound like when it is sung in school. Check: http://youtu.be/tD4MeAdbSMw

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri May 20, 2011 12:16 pm oh you Anno. setting apocalyptic events to kindergarten and school songs screwing with audiences

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri May 20, 2011 12:30 pm Xard wrote: oh you Anno. setting apocalyptic events to kindergarten and school songs

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri May 20, 2011 12:41 pm And what would that "significance" / assotiations be?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri May 20, 2011 12:48 pm Kendrix wrote: And what would that "significance" / assotiations be?

[]:             Quote Post by LoCooLaidman » Fri May 20, 2011 12:49 pm symbv wrote: Just think of a very famous school song with similar mood and imagine it being played in that context in the movie. Would it somewhat change your perception of the scenes?
